problem_statement: A free-form model response is too brittle for a planning surface that must later render ingredients, instructions, pantry coverage, and save actions. Without a strict generation contract, the feature will fail unpredictably and create extra parsing work in the UI.
in_scope: Add a server-side OpenAI integration that requests recipe drafts from pantry context using the Responses API
Define a strict response schema for generated recipe drafts including pantry-used ingredients missing ingredients meal type servings and instructions
Validate returned drafts before exposing them to any client component
requirements: The OpenAI API key must remain server-only
The generation path must reject malformed or incomplete model output before it reaches the client
The initial model choice must be configurable with a low-latency default suitable for interactive use
acceptance_criteria: A server action or route can return structured AI recipe drafts for the current pantry context
Drafts include explicit pantry-used and missing ingredient lists instead of only prose descriptions
Provider errors invalid JSON or schema validation failures return a controlled error result
dependencies: OpenAI API credentials for the deployment environment
risks: Model quality may drift if the prompt does not strongly constrain pantry usage
Token usage may rise if the full pantry is sent without trimming low-signal metadata
implementation_notes: Use the Responses API with structured output rather than a chat UI. Start with a configurable gpt-5-mini default and keep the response schema close to src/lib/recipe-schemas.ts so later persistence remains straightforward.
